Title: llvmlite.binding.TargetMachine.emit_object() creates incorrect ...
URL: https://github.com/numba/llvmlite/issues/181
Content: ## Navigation Menu. # Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests... # Provide feedback. We read every piece of feedback, and take your input very seriously. # Saved searches. ## Use saved searches to filter your results more quickly. To see all available qualifiers, see our documentation. # llvmlite.binding.TargetMachine.emit\_object() creates incorrect executable type on Windows #181. ## Footer. I was messing around and trying to create an executable using llvmlite on my Windows machine, but I couldn't get it to work. I noticed that even though all of the default settings told me that the executable would be of the COFF format, it was an ELF executable. Here's an example shell run showing the issue:  . This generated file cannot be run by Windows. I'm running Windows 10, the beta that allows Bash on Windows, and Python 3.5 in a Conda Virtual Environment. I'm using the latest llvmlite version available on Conda. ### Type. ## Issue actions. ### Footer navigation.

Title: Adding a new LLVM target to llvmlite - Stack Overflow

Content: Your python code asks llvmlite to create_target_machine and then calls LLVM's pass manager via existing llvmlite calls, which in turn calls the

Title: Target information — llvmlite 0.49.0dev0+18.g6302ab2.dirty documentation
URL: https://llvmlite.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user-guide/binding/target-information.html
Content: Target information allows you to inspect and modify aspects of the code generation, such as which CPU is targeted or what optimization level is desired. Minimal use of this module would be to create a `TargetMachine` for later use in code generation. from  llvmlite  import binding target = binding. :   Return a string representing the default target triple that LLVM is configured to produce code for. :   Return a target triple suitable for generating code for the current process. :   Get the object format for the given *triple* string, or the default triple if `None`. :   Get the name of the host’s CPU as a string. The returned value is an instance of the `FeatureMap` class, which adds a new method `.flatten()` for returning a string suitable for use as the `features` argument to `Target.create_target_machine()`. :   Create a new `Target` instance for the default platform that LLVM is configured to produce code for.

Title: llvmlite · PyPI
URL: https://pypi.org/project/llvmlite
Content: ## A Lightweight LLVM Python Binding for Writing JIT Compilers. llvmlite is a project originally tailored for Numba’s needs, using the. * A small C wrapper around the parts of the LLVM C++ API we need that are. not already exposed by the LLVM C API. * A ctypes Python wrapper around the C API. * A pure Python implementation of the subset of the LLVM IR builder that we. The old llvmpy binding exposes a lot of LLVM APIs but the mapping of. * The IR builder is pure Python code and decoupled from LLVM’s. * Most of llvmlite uses the LLVM C API which is small but very stable. * The binding is not a Python C-extension, but a plain DLL accessed using. | 0.34.0 - 0.36.0 | 10.0.x (9.0.x for aarch64 only) |. Details for the file `llvmlite-0.47.0.tar.gz`. llvmlite-0.47.0-cp314-cp314t-win\_amd64.whl. Details for the file `llvmlite-0.47.0-cp314-cp314t-win_amd64.whl`. Details for the file `llvmlite-0.47.0-cp314-cp314t-manylinux_2_27_aarch64.manylinux_2_28_aarch64.whl`. Details for the file `llvmlite-0.47.0-cp314-cp314t-manylinux2014_x86_64.manylinux_2_17_x86_64.whl`. Details for the file `llvmlite-0.47.0-cp314-cp314t-macosx_12_0_arm64.whl`.
